title: Transit Police seek public’s assistance in reported sexual assault investigation
content:
Metro Vancouver Transit Police are investigating an alleged sexual assault that occurred in Vancouver. Investigators are seeking dashcam footage from motorists who travelled on Stainsbury Avenue between Victoria Drive and Walker Street between 3:30 p.m. to 3:55 p.m. on Tuesday, May 26, 2026. They are also looking to speak to anyone who may have been walking in the area during that time.
Anyone who was in the area, or who has dashcam video is asked to contact Transit Police by phone at 604.515.8300, or by text at 87.77.77 and quote file number 2026-13363.
Metro Vancouver Transit Police do not believe there is an increased risk to the public. This investigation is ongoing and no further information is available at this time.

title: Transit Police arrest leads to 25 charges, many related to firearm offences
content:
Following an arrest by Metro Vancouver Transit Police, the BC Prosecution Service has approved multiple firearms related charges against a suspect from Surrey.
Sean Robert Gullick, age 33, is now facing 25 approved charges, most related to weapons offences under the Criminal Code of Canada. He remains in custody, pending his next court appearance. The charges include:
•5 x Possession of a controlled substance for the purposes of trafficking
•3 x Possession of firearms while prohibited
•3 x Possession of firearms without licence and/or registration
•2 x Possession of prohibited firearms
•2 x Careless storage of firearms
•Unauthorized possession of firearms
•Identity theft
•Possession of forgery instruments
•Possession of stolen property under $5000
•Unauthorized use of credit card data
•Deal with identity document without lawful excuse
•Breach of release order
•Assault with weapon
•Possession of a weapon for dangerous purpose
•Obstruct police officer
“Through the use of intelligence and targeted policing in areas of concern, the Transit Police Crime Suppression Team takes on serious crime along the transit system. We are proud that their hard work in this case has resulted in a dangerous individual and multiple firearms being taken off of the transit system.” – Constable Sunny Kainth

title: Transit Police appealing for witnesses in Joyce SkyTrain Station incident
content:
Metro Vancouver Transit Police is appealing for witnesses after an incident took place near the Joyce Skytrain Station.
On May 6, 2026 at 7:45p.m., Metro Vancouver Transit Police received a report of a male with a weapon, uttering threats near Joyce Skytrain Station.
Transit Police members located the male inside the station. During the interaction police used less lethal force options. The male attempted to leave the scene via the SkyTrain track area before falling to the ground below, after which he was taken into custody.
BC Emergency Health Services attended and the male was transported to local hospital with serious injuries.
Transit Police immediately notified the Independent Investigations Office of BC of the incident and is unable to provide additional information while the IIO’s independent process is taking place.
The Metro Vancouver Transit Police are requesting any witnesses who observed or had contact with the male prior to police arrival to contact 604-515-8300 and quote file # GV26-11349.

title: *Update* Three suspects in-custody after woman slashed at Burnaby SkyTrain station
content:
Update issued 23 April 2026
One of the suspects arrested by Metro Vancouver Transit Police, 39-year-old Richard Hoff of Abbotsford, has now be charged with unlawfully causing bodily harm by the BC Prosecution Service. Hoff has been remanded in-custody. His next court appearance is scheduled for April 24 2026.
Original media release, issued 05 September 2025
Three suspects have been arrested by Metro Vancouver Transit Police after a woman was slashed at Metrotown SkyTrain Station in Burnaby.
On Friday, September 5, 2025, just before noon, a physical altercation broke out inside the Station. A 70-year-old woman, who was standing nearby and not involved in the dispute, was slashed in the leg with an edged weapon during the fight. The individuals who were involved in the fight then fled the scene. Two men and a woman were later arrested and two discarded weapons, believed to be involved, were recovered nearby.
BC Emergency Health Services attended and transported the woman to hospital.
All three suspects are currently in custody.
“We are still in the early stages of the investigation, but we do not believe the victim and the suspects knew each other. This violent incident has had a significant impact on a woman who should be able to safely ride transit.” – Constable Amanda Steed
Anyone who may have witnessed this incident, and has not yet spoken to police, is asked to call 604.515.8300 or text 87.77.77

title: Charges approved in armed robbery and flight from Transit Police
content:
Following his arrest by Metro Vancouver Transit Police officers, a suspect has now been charged in an armed robbery that took place in Vancouver last month.
On March 26
th
, 2026, at approximately 7:20 p.m., Transit Police responded to a broadcast made by Vancouver Police Department about a robbery where a store employee had been threatened with a knife, which occurred near Granville SkyTrain Station. Officers quickly located the suspect and attempted to take him into custody. He fled, and then resisted arrest when apprehended, injuring one of the police officers in the process.
Jake Rupert Daniels of Vancouver, age 41, has been charged with Robbery, Obstruction and Failure to Comply with a Probation Order by the BC Prosecution Service. He has been released from custody with court-imposed conditions.
“Transit Police officers were nearby and able to intervene immediately. The officer who was injured during this incident was assessed at the hospital by medical staff, and is still off work. We are grateful that no members of the public were injured.” – Constable Sunny Kainth

title: Transit Police issue over $98k in fines during Distracted Driving Prevention Month
content:
The Metro Vancouver Transit Police Targeted Mobile Enforcement Team (TMET) issued 346 violation tickets during 23 distracted driving enforcement initiatives in and around major transit hubs, in support of ICBC’s Distracted Driving Awareness Month campaign.
Throughout the month of March, Transit Police TMET targeted distracted drivers who posed a threat to pedestrians making their way to and from transit stations and bus loops across Metro Vancouver. Enforcement took place at transit hubs including Brentwood, Lougheed, Coquitlam Central, Aberdeen, Main Street Station, Guildford and Newton, among others.
TMET was joined in these efforts by BC Highway Patrol, Burnaby RCMP, North Vancouver RCMP, Richmond RCMP, Vancouver Police Department, and West Vancouver Police Department.
During the month of March, TMET issued 267 Provincial violation tickets to people for using electronic devices while driving, resulting in $98,256 in fines. Officers also issued 79 additional violation tickets to drivers for a variety of offences, including: Drive Contrary to Restrictions, No Driver’s License, Drive without Insurance, and impaired driving. Numerous other drivers were given warnings and educated about the dangers of distracted driving.
“When you’re distracted, your reaction times are slow. You are 3.6 times more likely to crash if you’re on your phone while driving, and 40% of distracted driving related crashes occur at intersections. This creates an incredibly dangerous environment for people who are walking to and from transit stations, bus loops, or bus stops. Everyone deserves to feel safe during their transit journey, and that includes being safe on the way to and from transit.” – Sergeant Steve Newton

title: Transit Police arrest man with loaded gun at SkyTrain Station
content:
A 55-year-old man from Surrey remains in custody after Metro Vancouver Transit Police arrested him with a loaded firearm at Gateway SkyTrain Station, earlier this week.
On Monday, March 23, 2026, just after 10:00 a.m., Transit Police officers were checking passengers for fares within the Fare Paid Zone at Gateway SkyTrain Station in Surrey. They watched a man follow another passenger through the gates while failing to tap any Compass media or provide proof of payment. The resulting interaction revealed several outstanding warrants, and a loaded handgun and ammunition.
Roger David Mowat has been charged with one count of Possession of a Loaded Prohibited or Restricted Firearm. He remains in custody pending his next court appearance.
“In policing, routine tasks can have dramatic outcomes. In this case, simply ensuring that transit users had valid fare while using the system resulted in a dangerous weapon being removed from transit before any harm could come to the public.” – Constable Amanda Steed

title: Transit Police seize weapons, drugs in three-month long crackdown at Surrey Central Station
content:
New Westminster
— A Metro Vancouver Transit Police-led project has resulted in numerous arrests, and a variety of weapons and drugs seized, in Surrey.
The three-month project launched on November 22, 2025, with police officers from the Transit Police Crime Suppression Team, Surrey RCMP, Surrey Police Service and the Combined Forces Special Enforcement Unit-BC working together. Designed to address crime and safety concerns, the project focused on Surrey Central Station and adjacent businesses, including the Central City complex.
As a result of the targeted police action:
61 arrests were made for reasons ranging from outstanding warrants, weapons possession, theft, trafficking a controlled substance, and Motor Vehicle Act violation
18 weapons were seized, including 12 knives and 5 cannisters of bear spray
258 grams of fentanyl, 38 grams of methamphetamine, 42 grams of cocaine, 298 pills of hydromorphone and 178 oxycontin pills were seized
$9,400 CAD believed to be the proceeds of crime was seized
$2,000 in stolen merchandise was recovered
The project, made possible through Provincial Special Investigation and Targeted Enforcement (SITE) Funding, demonstrates the way in which Transit Police utilizes intelligence reports, crime data and reports from the public in order to strategically focus police resources in partnership with policing and community partners.
“Surrey Central Station is a busy transit hub. SkyTrain and bus passengers deserve to feel safe without concern about being exposed to weapons, drugs, or the violence that can sometimes accompany them. It’s Transit Police’s aim that anyone who brings these items to transit be arrested and held accountable.” – Constable Sunny Kainth

title: Transit Police investigate vandalized bus shelters
content:
Metro Vancouver Transit Police are currently investigating an increase in vandalism to bus shelters across several municipalities including Burnaby, New Westminster, and Surrey.
On February 2, 2026, Transit Police were notified by Coast Mountain Bus Company that a number of bus shelters had been vandalized along 152
nd
Street, between 26
th
Avenue and 86
th
Avenue, in Surrey. Fourteen bus shelters were located with their glass shattered by, what is believed to be a small projectile. Since that time, Transit Police’s Investigative Units have identified similar vandalism in municipalities throughout Metro Vancouver. To date, three dozen bus shelters have been identified that share similar patterns of vandalism.
We are asking anyone with information or video footage of the incidents to come forward and speak with investigators by calling 604.515.8300 or texting 87.77.77. Alternately, anonymous tips can be submitted to Crime Stoppers by calling 1.800.222.8477 or online at
solvecrime.ca

title: Transit Police seek public’s assistance in sexual assault in Surrey
content:
Metro Vancouver Transit Police are investigating a report of a sexual assault that occurred at the Scott Road Park and Ride Lot A, adjacent to Scott Road SkyTrain Station in North Surrey. After exhausting all investigative avenues, we are asking witnesses, or anyone with dash-cam video, to contact police.
On January 21, 2026, just after 4:00 p.m., a woman was walking from SkyTrain to her car, parked in the far corner of the parking lot, near 110 Avenue and 126A Street. As she arrived at her car, she was approached by a man from behind and sexually assaulted. The woman fought back and the suspect fled toward the station.
The suspect is described as a Caucasian man, in his mid-to-late twenties, tall, with a medium build, light coloured eyes, partial brown beard, wearing a blue zip up hoodie with the hood pulled up, dark blue jeans and a black toque.
Anyone who witnessed the assault, noticed anything suspicious in the area, or has dash-cam video from the Park and Ride between 3:45 p.m. and 4:30 p.m., is asked to contact Transit Police by phone at 604.515.8300, or by text at 87.77.77 and quote file number 2026-3590.
